title: Donut maps with DonutMap
text: input data tidy: row gives value one category one location. Locations can supplied either longitude latitude columns sf object. background map, vignette crops Natural Earth Canada boundary eastern Canada. donut values flow values remain simulated.
code: sites <- data.frame(   site = c("Site A", "Site B", "Site C", "Site D", "Site E"),   lon = c(-73.57, -71.21, -72.75, -68.52, -66.82),   lat = c(45.50, 46.81, 45.40, 48.45, 50.22) )  categories <- c("Walking", "Transit", "Car")  demo <- merge(   sites,   data.frame(category = categories),   by = NULL )  demo$value <- c(   32, 48, 120,   55, 80, 95,   28, 70, 110,   20, 44, 76,   18, 30, 58 )  demo$category <- factor(demo$category, levels = categories)  flows <- data.frame(   from = c("Site A", "Site A", "Site B", "Site C", "Site D"),   to = c("Site B", "Site C", "Site D", "Site E", "Site E"),   trips = c(180, 90, 120, 70, 60) )  category_colours <- c(   Walking = "#1b9e77",   Transit = "#7570b3",   Car = "#d95f02" ) canada <- rnaturalearth::ne_countries(   country = "Canada",   returnclass = "sf" )  eastern_canada <- sf::st_crop(   canada,   sf::st_bbox(     c(xmin = -81, ymin = 44, xmax = -62, ymax = 53.5),     crs = sf::st_crs(4326)   ) ) #> Warning: attribute variables are assumed to be spatially constant throughout #> all geometries

title: Donut maps with DonutMap
text: donut_map() returns normal ggplot object, additional ggplot2 layers, scales, labels, themes can added afterwards. flows argument adds links donut locations. Use flow_curvature = 0 straight links, larger positive negative values curved trajectories.
code: donut_map(   demo,   site,   category,   value,   lon = lon,   lat = lat,   map = eastern_canada,   crs = 3347,   radius_range = c(25000, 70000),   colours = category_colours,   flows = flows,   from = from,   to = to,   flow_value = trips,   flow_linewidth_range = c(0.3, 2.2),   flow_curvature = 0.22,   flow_arrow = TRUE ) +   labs(     title = "Simulated mobility composition by site",     fill = "Mode",     linewidth = "Trips"   ) +   theme(legend.position = "right")

title: Donut maps with DonutMap
text: donut_leaflet() returns leaflet htmlwidget. Donut segments trajectory lines, including arrowheads, can clicked open popups, hover labels enabled default. default, interactive donuts constructed EPSG:3857, display projection used Leaflet, keeps sector boundaries visually regular screen.
code: donut_leaflet(   demo,   site,   category,   value,   lon = lon,   lat = lat,   map = eastern_canada,   radius_range = c(25000, 70000),   colours = category_colours,   flows = flows,   from = from,   to = to,   flow_value = trips,   flow_weight_range = c(1, 7),   flow_curvature = 0.22,   flow_arrow = TRUE,   flow_arrow_size = 45000,   flow_colour = "#1f2937",   flow_opacity = 0.75 )

title: Donut maps with DonutMap
text: flow_lines() returns trajectory layer directly sf object. useful want build custom map layer inspect geometry plotting.
code: trajectories <- flow_lines(   flows,   demo,   from,   to,   trips,   site,   lon = lon,   lat = lat,   crs = 3347,   flow_curvature = 0.22,   flow_n = 40 )  trajectories #> Simple feature collection with 5 features and 3 fields #> Geometry type: LINESTRING #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: 7631610 ymin: 1244380 xmax: 7933770 ymax: 1910206 #> Projected CRS: NAD83 / Statistics Canada Lambert #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>   from   to     value                                                   geometry #>   <chr>  <chr>  <dbl>                                           <LINESTRING [m]> #> 1 Site A Site B   180 (7631610 1244380, 7632825 1250853, 7634155 1257251, 76355… #> 2 Site A Site C    90 (7631610 1244380, 7633203 1245308, 7634801 1246199, 76364… #> 3 Site B Site D   120 (7763098 1440475, 7763754 1448083, 7764551 1455617, 77654… #> 4 Site C Site E    70 (7697210 1252459, 7696045 1271925, 7695261 1291254, 76948… #> 5 Site D Site E    60 (7892189 1681853, 7890745 1688166, 7889433 1694454, 78882…

title: Donut maps with DonutMap
text: specialized workflows, donut_polygons() returns donut segments valid sf polygons. can use object package accepts sf polygons.
code: donuts <- donut_polygons(   demo,   site,   category,   value,   lon = lon,   lat = lat,   crs = 3347,   radius_range = c(25000, 70000) )  donuts #> Simple feature collection with 15 features and 8 fields #> Geometry type: POLYGON #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: 7590543 ymin: 1182476 xmax: 7963944 ymax: 1940386 #> Projected CRS: NAD83 / Statistics Canada Lambert #> # A tibble: 15 × 9 #>    id     category value total proportion radius start_angle end_angle #>    <chr>  <fct>    <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl>  <dbl>       <dbl>     <dbl> #>  1 Site A Walking     32   171     0.187  41076.       1.57      0.395 #>  2 Site A Transit     95   171     0.556  41076.       0.395    -3.10  #>  3 Site A Car         44   171     0.257  41076.      -3.10     -4.71  #>  4 Site B Walking     48   152     0.316  25000        1.57     -0.413 #>  5 Site B Transit     28   152     0.184  25000       -0.413    -1.57  #>  6 Site B Car         76   152     0.5    25000       -1.57     -4.71  #>  7 Site C Walking    120   208     0.577  70000        1.57     -2.05  #>  8 Site C Transit     70   208     0.337  70000       -2.05     -4.17  #>  9 Site C Car         18   208     0.0865 70000       -4.17     -4.71  #> 10 Site D Walking     55   195     0.282  60155.       1.57     -0.201 #> 11 Site D Transit    110   195     0.564  60155.      -0.201    -3.75  #> 12 Site D Car         30   195     0.154  60155.      -3.75     -4.71  #> 13 Site E Walking     80   158     0.506  30180.       1.57     -1.61  #> 14 Site E Transit     20   158     0.127  30180.      -1.61     -2.41  #> 15 Site E Car         58   158     0.367  30180.      -2.41     -4.71  #> # ℹ 1 more variable: geometry <POLYGON [m]> plot(donuts["category"])

title: Authors and Citation
text: Aurélien Nicosia. Author, maintainer.

text: Nicosia (2026). DonutMap: Donut Maps 'sf', 'ggplot2', 'leaflet'. R package version 0.0.0.9000, https://aureliennicosiaulaval.github.io/DonutMap/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {DonutMap: Donut Maps with 'sf', 'ggplot2', and 'leaflet'},   author = {Aurélien Nicosia},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://aureliennicosiaulaval.github.io/DonutMap/}, }

title: DonutMap
text: DonutMap R package drawing donut charts static interactive maps sf, ggplot2, leaflet. Website: https://aureliennicosiaulaval.github.io/DonutMap/ package inspired mtennekes/donutmaps, starts simpler tidy-data interface avoids older odf/tmap workflow.

title: DonutMap
code: # From GitHub devtools::install_github("AurelienNicosiaULaval/DonutMap")  # From this local repository devtools::install()

title: DonutMap
text: donut_map() creates static ggplot2 map optional curved trajectories arrows. donut_leaflet() creates interactive leaflet map clickable donut segments, popups, hover labels, legends, optional curved trajectories directional arrowheads. builds interactive donut symbols EPSG:3857 default sector separators stay visually regular Leaflet. donut_polygons() computes sf polygon layer one donut segment per non-zero location-category pair. flow_lines() computes straight curved origin-destination sf line geometries.

title: DonutMap
code: library(DonutMap) library(ggplot2)  demo <- data.frame(   place = rep(c("A", "B", "C"), each = 3),   lon = rep(c(-71.35, -71.20, -71.05), each = 3),   lat = rep(c(46.75, 46.82, 46.73), each = 3),   category = rep(c("Walking", "Transit", "Car"), times = 3),   value = c(10, 20, 5, 5, 15, 10, 12, 4, 9) )  flows <- data.frame(   from = c("A", "B"),   to = c("B", "C"),   trips = c(30, 10) )  mode_colours <- c(   Walking = "#1b9e77",   Transit = "#7570b3",   Car = "#d95f02" )

title: DonutMap
code: donut_map(   demo,   place,   category,   value,   lon = lon,   lat = lat,   flows = flows,   from = from,   to = to,   flow_value = trips,   flow_curvature = 0.22,   flow_arrow = TRUE,   colours = mode_colours )

title: DonutMap
text: Use flow_curvature = 0 straight links, positive values one bend direction, negative values opposite direction. flow_arrow = TRUE adds directional arrows static interactive trajectories. donut_leaflet(), use flow_arrow_size tune arrowhead length projected map units automatic size ideal.
code: donut_leaflet(   demo,   place,   category,   value,   lon = lon,   lat = lat,   flows = flows,   from = from,   to = to,   flow_value = trips,   flow_curvature = 0.22,   flow_arrow = TRUE,   colours = mode_colours )

title: DonutMap
text: pkgdown site includes complete vignette : simulated Québec/eastern Canada example data; static ggplot2 donut map directional trajectories; interactive leaflet donut map clickable directional trajectories; direct use sf geometry layer. See vignette("donut-maps", package = "DonutMap") locally, online article: https://aureliennicosiaulaval.github.io/DonutMap/articles/donut-maps.html

what: DonutMap: donut maps with sf and ggplot2 — DonutMap-package
title: DonutMap: donut maps with sf and ggplot2 — DonutMap-package
text: DonutMap creates donut charts positioned map tidy data. main static entry point donut_map(). main interactive entry point donut_leaflet(). Use donut_polygons() need sf polygon layer directly, flow_lines() need origin-destination line trajectory geometries.

title: DonutMap: donut maps with sf and ggplot2 — DonutMap-package
text: Maintainer: Aurélien Nicosia 116816597+AurelienNicosiaULaval@users.noreply.github.com

what: Draw an interactive donut map — donut_leaflet
title: Draw an interactive donut map — donut_leaflet
text: donut_leaflet() returns leaflet htmlwidget clickable donut segments, optional origin-destination links curved trajectories, popups, labels, legend, layer controls.

title: Draw an interactive donut map — donut_leaflet
code: donut_leaflet(   data,   id,   category,   value,   map = NULL,   lon = NULL,   lat = NULL,   input_crs = 4326,   crs = 3857,   radius_range = NULL,   inner_radius = 0.55,   n = 96,   colours = NULL,   flows = NULL,   from = NULL,   to = NULL,   flow_value = NULL,   flow_min = NULL,   flow_weight_range = c(1, 8),   flow_curvature = 0.18,   flow_n = 30,   flow_arrow = TRUE,   flow_arrow_size = NULL,   flow_colour = "grey35",   flow_opacity = 0.55,   provider_tiles = "CartoDB.Positron",   popup = TRUE,   label = TRUE,   prefer_canvas = FALSE,   map_fill = "#f3f4f6",   map_colour = "#ffffff",   map_weight = 1,   map_opacity = 0.9,   donut_colour = "#ffffff",   donut_weight = 1,   donut_opacity = 0.9 )

title: Draw an interactive donut map — donut_leaflet
text: data data frame sf object. row one category one donut location. id Unquoted column identifying donut location. category Unquoted column identifying donut categories. value Unquoted numeric column giving non-negative category values. map Optional sf object used background layer. lon, lat Unquoted longitude latitude columns. Required data sf object. input_crs Coordinate reference system lon lat, sf object missing CRS. Defaults EPSG:4326. crs Target projected CRS used build interactive donut trajectory geometries. Defaults EPSG:3857, Leaflet's default display projection, donut circles sector separators remain visually regular screen. radius_range Numeric vector length 2 giving minimum maximum donut radii map units. NULL, range derived map extent. inner_radius Numeric value (0, 1) giving inner radius proportion outer radius. n Number points used approximate complete outer circle. colours Optional category colours. Use named vector stable category-colour matching. flows Optional data frame origin-destination flows. , Unquoted columns flows identifying origin destination ids. Required flows supplied. flow_value Optional unquoted numeric column flows used scale line widths. omitted, flow receives value 1. flow_min Optional minimum flow value draw. flow_weight_range Numeric vector length 2 controlling interactive flow line weights. flow_curvature Numeric curvature trajectory lines. Use 0 straight lines, positive values one bend direction, negative values opposite direction. flow_n Number points used approximate curved trajectory. flow_arrow interactive flow trajectories include arrowheads? flow_arrow_size Arrowhead length projected map units. NULL, size derived donut radii. flow_colour Flow line colour. flow_opacity Flow line opacity. provider_tiles Leaflet provider tiles. Use NULL skip tile layers. popup popups attached donut segments flow lines? label hover labels attached donut segments flow lines? prefer_canvas Leaflet prefer Canvas SVG vector rendering? default FALSE gives crisper small donut separators. map_fill, map_colour Background map fill outline colours. map_weight Background map border weight. map_opacity Background map opacity. donut_colour Donut segment border colour. donut_weight Donut segment border weight. donut_opacity Donut segment fill opacity.

title: Draw an interactive donut map — donut_leaflet
text: leaflet htmlwidget.

title: Draw an interactive donut map — donut_leaflet
Draw a donut map — donut_map
text: donut_map() returns ggplot2 map donut charts located top optional sf background map. can also add origin-destination links curved trajectories donut locations.

title: Draw a donut map — donut_map
code: donut_map(   data,   id,   category,   value,   map = NULL,   lon = NULL,   lat = NULL,   input_crs = 4326,   crs = NULL,   radius_range = NULL,   inner_radius = 0.55,   n = 96,   colours = NULL,   flows = NULL,   from = NULL,   to = NULL,   flow_value = NULL,   flow_min = NULL,   flow_linewidth_range = c(0.2, 2.5),   flow_curvature = 0.18,   flow_n = 30,   flow_arrow = TRUE,   flow_arrow_length = 0.12,   flow_colour = "grey35",   flow_alpha = 0.45,   map_fill = "grey96",   map_colour = "white",   donut_colour = "white",   donut_linewidth = 0.15 )

title: Draw a donut map — donut_map
text: data data frame sf object. row one category one donut location. id Unquoted column identifying donut location. category Unquoted column identifying donut categories. value Unquoted numeric column giving non-negative category values. map Optional sf object used background layer. lon, lat Unquoted longitude latitude columns. Required data sf object. input_crs Coordinate reference system lon lat, sf object missing CRS. Defaults EPSG:4326. crs Target projected CRS used build map. radius_range Numeric vector length 2 giving minimum maximum donut radii map units. NULL, range derived map extent. inner_radius Numeric value (0, 1) giving inner radius proportion outer radius. n Number points used approximate complete outer circle. colours Optional category colours. Use named vector stable category-colour matching. flows Optional data frame origin-destination flows. , Unquoted columns flows identifying origin destination ids. Required flows supplied. flow_value Optional unquoted numeric column flows used scale line widths. omitted, flow receives value 1. flow_min Optional minimum flow value draw. flow_linewidth_range Numeric vector length 2 controlling flow line widths. flow_curvature Numeric curvature trajectory lines. Use 0 straight lines, positive values one bend direction, negative values opposite direction. flow_n Number points used approximate curved trajectory. flow_arrow static flow trajectories include arrows? flow_arrow_length Arrow length inches flow_arrow = TRUE. flow_colour, flow_alpha Flow line colour alpha. map_fill, map_colour Background map fill outline colours. donut_colour, donut_linewidth Donut segment border colour linewidth.

title: Draw a donut map — donut_map
text: ggplot object.

title: Draw a donut map — donut_map
code: demo <- data.frame(   place = rep(c("A", "B", "C"), each = 3),   lon = rep(c(-71.35, -71.2, -71.05), each = 3),   lat = rep(c(46.75, 46.82, 46.73), each = 3),   category = rep(c("x", "y", "z"), times = 3),   value = c(10, 20, 5, 5, 15, 10, 12, 4, 9) )  flows <- data.frame(   from = c("A", "B"),   to = c("B", "C"),   trips = c(30, 10) )  donut_map(   demo,   place,   category,   value,   lon = lon,   lat = lat,   flows = flows,   from = from,   to = to,   flow_value = trips )

title: Compute donut polygons — donut_polygons
text: donut_polygons() turns tidy category values spatial locations sf polygon layer. row data represents one category one location.

title: Compute donut polygons — donut_polygons
code: donut_polygons(   data,   id,   category,   value,   lon = NULL,   lat = NULL,   input_crs = 4326,   crs = NULL,   map = NULL,   radius_range = NULL,   inner_radius = 0.55,   n = 96,   start_angle = pi/2 )

title: Compute donut polygons — donut_polygons
text: data data frame sf object. data sf object, lon lat must supplied. id Unquoted column identifying donut location. category Unquoted column identifying donut categories. value Unquoted numeric column giving non-negative category values. lon, lat Unquoted longitude latitude columns. Required data sf object. input_crs Coordinate reference system lon lat, sf object missing CRS. Defaults EPSG:4326. crs Target projected CRS used build donut polygons. NULL, projected CRS chosen map, data, estimated UTM zone. map Optional sf object used choose working CRS default donut radius range. radius_range Numeric vector length 2 giving minimum maximum donut radii map units. NULL, range derived map extent. inner_radius Numeric value (0, 1) giving inner radius proportion outer radius. n Number points used approximate complete outer circle. start_angle Start angle radians. default starts 12 o'clock.

title: Compute donut polygons — donut_polygons
text: sf object one polygon per non-zero location-category pair.

title: Compute donut polygons — donut_polygons
code: demo <- data.frame(   place = rep(c("A", "B"), each = 3),   lon = rep(c(-71.3, -71.1), each = 3),   lat = rep(c(46.75, 46.85), each = 3),   category = rep(c("x", "y", "z"), times = 2),   value = c(10, 20, 5, 5, 15, 10) )  donuts <- donut_polygons(demo, place, category, value, lon = lon, lat = lat) plot(donuts["category"])

title: Compute origin-destination flow lines — flow_lines
text: flow_lines() creates sf line geometries joining origin destination locations. Lines can straight curved trajectories.

title: Compute origin-destination flow lines — flow_lines
code: flow_lines(   flows,   locations,   from,   to,   value = NULL,   id,   lon = NULL,   lat = NULL,   input_crs = 4326,   crs = NULL,   drop_self = TRUE,   flow_curvature = 0,   flow_n = 30 )

title: Compute origin-destination flow lines — flow_lines
text: flows data frame containing origin-destination pairs. locations data frame sf object containing one rows per location. Repeated locations reduced first geometry per id. , Unquoted columns flows identifying origin destination ids. value Optional unquoted numeric column flows used flow value. omitted, flow receives value 1. id Unquoted location id column locations. lon, lat Unquoted longitude latitude columns locations. Required locations sf object. input_crs Coordinate reference system lon lat, sf object missing CRS. Defaults EPSG:4326. crs Target projected CRS. NULL, projected CRS selected locations estimated UTM zone. drop_self self-flows removed? flow_curvature Numeric curvature trajectory lines. Use 0 straight lines, positive values one bend direction, negative values opposite direction. flow_n Number points used approximate curved trajectory.

title: Compute origin-destination flow lines — flow_lines
text: sf object one line per retained flow.

title: Compute origin-destination flow lines — flow_lines
code: locations <- data.frame(   place = c("A", "B"),   lon = c(-71.3, -71.1),   lat = c(46.75, 46.85) ) flows <- data.frame(from = "A", to = "B", trips = 15)  lines <- flow_lines(flows, locations, from, to, trips, place, lon = lon, lat = lat) plot(lines["value"])
